deep_dive.title: SingGuard-NSFA: Extensible Guardrails for Agentic AI via Generative Reasoning and Real-Time Classification
deep_dive.abstract: We present nsfaguard, a guardrail framework for securing agentic AI systems against operational threats, such as prompt injection, sensitive information extraction, malicious code requests, dangerous tool misuse, and resource exhaustion. We first introduce the NSFA taxonomy, which organizes 185 risk variants into a CIA-triad-grounded hierarchy and is cross-validated against three well-established OWASP guidelines. Based on this taxonomy, we construct a benchmark suite spanning 133 languages, comprising over 93K purpose-built samples targeting both user queries and agent responses, along with 3,435 cross-source samples adapted from five public agent-security datasets. To detect these operational threats in practice, we develop a dual-mode approach combining SFT-based generative reasoning for interpretable offline auditing with discriminative classification heads on the frozen backbone, enabling real-time detection at approximately 50,ms. We release four models with 0.8B, 2B, 4B, and 9B parameters, all achieving $\geq$94% F1 on purpose-built benchmarks and surpassing the strongest competing guardrails by 6 to 12 absolute points. On cross-source evaluation, the 9B model attains 91.29% F1 with a more balanced precision--recall trade-off. Moreover, ablation experiments show that classification heads can equip a guardrail with risk detection capabilities beyond its original scope and achieve state-of-the-art performance. These results demonstrate the extensibility of the approach and its generality as a plug-in enhancement.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: compact guardrail models (0.8B-9B) for real-time LLM safety classification
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 紧凑型护栏模型(0.8B-9B)用于实时LLM安全分类
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 紧凑型护栏模型(0.8B-9B)用于实时LLM安全分类
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 紧凑型护栏模型(0.8B-9B)用于实时LLM安全分类
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Existing guardrails lack extensibility and speed for agentic AI threats. SingGuard-NSFA proposes a dual-mode approach with SFT-based reasoning and discriminative classification heads on frozen backbones, releasing 0.8B-9B models achieving >=94% F1 and ~50ms real-time detection.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有智能体AI护栏缺乏可扩展性与实时检测能力。本文提出SingGuard-NSFA双模式框架，结合SFT生成推理与冻结骨干上的判别分类头，发布0.8B至9B四种参数规模的护栏模型，在自建基准上F1达94%以上，实时检测延迟约50毫秒，超越现有最强护栏6-12个绝对点，验证了小模型护栏的可扩展性与实用部署价值。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有智能体AI护栏缺乏可扩展性与实时检测能力。本文提出SingGuard-NSFA双模式框架，结合SFT生成推理与冻结骨干上的判别分类头，发布0.8B至9B四种参数规模的护栏模型，在自建基准上F1达94%以上，实时检测延迟约50毫秒，超越现有最强护栏6-12个绝对点，验证了小模型护栏的可扩展性与实用部署价值。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: compact efficient guard model for LLM input and output safety classification
deep_dive.public_resource_evidence: 3,435 cross-source samples adapted from five public agent-security datasets
deep_dive.quality_gate_reason_cn: 动机具体且强，提出小参数护栏模型用于实时安全分类并报告参数量与延迟；使用了公开的智能体安全数据集

quick_skim.title: When Words Are Safe But Actions Kill: Probing Physical Danger Beyond Text Safety in Hidden-State Risk Space
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly serve as high-level planners for embodied agents, where linguistically benign instructions can become unsafe once grounded in the physical world. We study whether this physically grounded danger is the same safety problem as ordinary text-level content danger. Through hidden-state direction analysis and random-split null tests, we show that content danger (CD) and physical danger (PD) form separable signals in LLM representations across Qwen2.5-3B/7B/14B/32B, Phi-3.5 and SmolLM2. Building on the CD/PD separability, we propose PRISM, a single-layer L2-regularized logistic probe over full hidden states. PRISM achieves 86.2--87.7\% accuracy on SafeAgentBench with 11.7--13.7\% FPR, while same-scale LLM judges over-block safe tasks at 24.7--39.0\% FPR. We further introduce PhysicalSafetyBench-1K (PSB-1K), a contrastive benchmark of 1{,}000 physical-risk pairs without direct harm keywords, to test whether methods detect physically grounded danger rather than explicit unsafe wording. On PSB-1K, PRISM reaches 99.6\% accuracy and 0.7\% FPR, whereas a Qwen2.5-3B judge rejects 67.8\% of safe tasks. PRISM also replicates on SafeText and EARBench, supporting hidden-state probing as a representation-level method for physical safety beyond text moderation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: single-layer logistic probe for physical danger classification with low FPR
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 单层逻辑探针用于物理危险分类且误报率低
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 单层逻辑探针用于物理危险分类且误报率低
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 单层逻辑探针用于物理危险分类且误报率低
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: LLMs as embodied planners can produce physically dangerous but textually benign instructions. PRISM uses a single-layer L2-regularized logistic probe on hidden states to separate content vs physical danger, achieving 86-88% accuracy on SafeAgentBench and 99.6% on PSB-1K with far lower FPR than LLM judges.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 大语言模型作为具身规划器时，语言上安全的指令可能在物理世界产生危险。现有LLM裁判对安全任务过度拦截。本文提出PRISM，利用隐藏状态中内容危险与物理危险的可分离性，训练单层L2正则化逻辑探针进行轻量安全分类。在SafeAgentBench上达到86.2-87.7%准确率，误报率仅11.7-13.7%，远低于同规模LLM裁判的24.7-39.0%。在PSB-1K上准确率达99.6%。该探针为轻量级LLM安全防护提供了高效低成本的表示层方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 大语言模型作为具身规划器时，语言上安全的指令可能在物理世界产生危险。现有LLM裁判对安全任务过度拦截。本文提出PRISM，利用隐藏状态中内容危险与物理危险的可分离性，训练单层L2正则化逻辑探针进行轻量安全分类。在SafeAgentBench上达到86.2-87.7%准确率，误报率仅11.7-13.7%，远低于同规模LLM裁判的24.7-39.0%。在PSB-1K上准确率达99.6%。该探针为轻量级LLM安全防护提供了高效低成本的表示层方案。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: small parameter-efficient safety classifier for large language model prompts and responses
quick_skim.quality_gate_reason_cn: 动机具体且非平凡，提出轻量探针替代LLM裁判进行安全分类，与需求3高度相关；SafeAgentBench为已知公开基准
